skill.name: delon-form-dynamic-schema-forms
skill.description: Create dynamic schema-based forms using @delon/form (SF component). Use this skill when building complex forms with validation, conditional rendering, async data loading, custom widgets, and multi-step workflows. Ensures forms follow JSON Schema standards, integrate with Angular reactive forms, support internationalization, and maintain consistent validation patterns across the application.
skill.summary: Build maintainable Angular dynamic forms with @delon/form schema and UI patterns.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found markdown TypeScript examples, Angular template literals, validation regexes, and public documentation links. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or executable installer behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: TypeScript Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are markdown code fences and Angular component template literals. They document @delon/form examples and do not run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside markdown TypeScript examples. There is no Ruby code, shell interpolation, subprocess API, or command string.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Form Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match words in descriptions, JSON Schema examples, or layout field names. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose and form schema property names. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms being used.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden File Access Is a Validation Regex
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding points to a password uppercase validator using a regular expression. It does not read hidden files or access the filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code calls a JavaScript regular expression test on a form value. There is no path, dotfile reference, file read, or file write.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs Are Public Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are references to @delon/form, JSON Schema, and SF component documentation. They are not runtime network calls or exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a References section. I found no fetch, HTTP client, webhook, credential transfer, or background network behavior.

content.value_statement: Dynamic Angular forms can become hard to validate and maintain. This skill guides @delon/form schemas, UI schemas, validators, async options, and wizard flows.
actual_capabilities: Creates @delon/form SF schema and UI schema patterns for Angular components.
Shows widget configuration for strings, numbers, dates, selects, uploads, and toggles.
Guides async option loading for dropdowns, autocomplete fields, and cascading selects.
Documents conditional field visibility, custom validators, and password confirmation checks.
Covers multi-step wizard forms, responsive grid layout, and custom form actions.
limitations: Does not provide runnable project scaffolding or package installation commands.
Examples require an Angular application with @delon/form and ng-alain dependencies.
Does not replace application-specific data services or backend validation.
Custom widgets beyond the documented examples still need local implementation.

use_cases.title: Create Admin Data Entry Forms
use_cases.description: Build schema-driven create and edit forms with validation, typed fields, and consistent layout.

use_cases.title: Standardize Enterprise Form Patterns
use_cases.description: Document shared @delon/form conventions for widgets, validators, async selects, and responsive grids.

use_cases.title: Prototype Workflow Wizards
use_cases.description: Model multi-step forms that collect data across stages and provide a review step before submission.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Schema Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an @delon/form SF schema for a user profile form with name, email, age, and role fields.

prompt_templates.title: Add Widgets and Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an @delon/form schema and UI schema for a product form with date, number, select, and textarea widgets.

prompt_templates.title: Build Async and Conditional Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an @delon/form pattern with async assignee loading and fields that change visibility based on user type.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Step Wizard
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a three-step @delon/form wizard with separate schemas, shared accumulated state, validation, and final review.

output_examples.input: A request for a customer onboarding form with contact fields and role selection.
output_examples.output: A form plan with required fields, validation rules, widget choices, and a matching UI schema layout.

output_examples.input: A request for dependent country and city selects.
output_examples.output: A cascading select design that loads countries first, updates selected state, and refreshes city options.

output_examples.input: A request for a registration wizard.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow outline with basic information, address details, confirmation, and final submit behavior.

best_practices: Keep validation rules in the schema whenever @delon/form supports them directly.
Use UI schema settings for layout and widget behavior instead of duplicating component code.
Test async option loading, conditional fields, and submit error paths before release.
anti_patterns: Do not put business authorization rules only in client-side form validation.
Do not hardcode production option lists when they must come from services.
Do not mix unrelated wizard step state without a clear accumulated data model.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Angular dynamic forms using @delon/form SF schemas, widgets, validators, and layouts.

faq.question: Does it include installation instructions?
faq.answer: No. It assumes the Angular project already has @delon/form and related ng-alain dependencies.

faq.question: Can it handle async dropdown data?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for async select options, autocomplete data, and cascading fields.

faq.question: Can it create wizard forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows multi-step form structure with separate schemas and accumulated state.

faq.question: Does it replace backend validation?
faq.answer: No. Backend validation is still required for security and data integrity.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
